channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Most Embedded CI Stops One Step Too Early
item.description: Software teams have spent years making CI feel normal. A pull request builds automatically. Unit tests run automatically. Static analysis, linting, packaging, and deployment checks all happen...

item.title: Why I built a page with no immediate growth upside, and shipped it anyway
item.description: XEdge has a discovery problem that's already solved. Describe a goal, get a stack, done. What it didn't have was a reason for anyone to come back after that first answer. A friend pointed this out...

item.title: The New Way to Showcase Software Skills
item.description: Two things are true about hiring in 2026, and they should not both be true at once. Job seekers say they are being ghosted at a scale they have never seen. Recruiters say they are drowning, buried...

item.title: My Checklist for Reviewing AI-Generated Code
item.description: The agent handed me a function that fetched a Stripe customer, read customer.tax_ids.data[0].value, and used it as the VAT number for an invoice. Clean code. Typed. Named well. It read perfectly....

item.title: RoomCraft AI: optimizar la distribución de una habitación con Simulated Annealing
item.description: Colocar los muebles de una habitación es un problema de optimización con muchas restricciones: la cama no va delante de la puerta, el escritorio quiere luz natural, hay que poder circular. Hay un...

item.title: Why heat pumps are still so hot in the US
item.description: It feels as if it should be illegal to even think about heating appliances during the height of summer—seriously, these heat waves in New York have been brutal—but we need to talk about heat...

item.title: Please Stop Making Me Opt Out of AI
item.description: I’m sick of “opt-out” toggles for automatically enabled generative AI features. It’s past time to make “opt in” the default setting for sensitive features.

item.title: 11 Open-Source Tools I Install on Every New Development Machine
item.description: Key Concepts 🗝️ These 11 tools have saved me thousands of hours over the past 4 years. If you're serious about becoming a better full-stack developer, they're worth mastering before chasing the...

item.title: Navigating Intellectual Property as a software Developer.
item.description: What I learned at Zone01 Kisumu's IP training Introduction Yesterday, I attended an Intellectual Property training at Zone01 Kisumu, and it fundamentally changed how I think about the code I...

item.title: MCP for AWS Security Engineers: Build a Read-Only Security Hub Triage Agent
item.description: MCP for AWS Security Engineers: Build a Read-Only Security Hub Triage Agent For AWS-heavy security work, I would start with AWS Agent Toolkit for AWS and the managed AWS MCP Server, not a custom...

item.title: How to Build an AI Agent with n8n
item.description: Building an AI agent with n8n is the fastest, cheapest way to turn a large language model into a useful worker — if you stay within its sweet spot. The honest truth, informed by the custom agents...

item.title: 5 Things I Learned Building a Chrome Extension That Watches ChatGPT, Claude & Gemini
item.description: I spent the last few months building a Chrome extension that detects HTML code blocks inside ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini and lets you deploy them straight to a live URL. The "deploy" part turned...

item.title: OnePlus officially gives up on the US and Europe
item.description: OnePlus has confirmed what industry observers have long expected: it's quitting the US and European markets, and will no longer launch new products in either region. Parent company Oppo promises...

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-Red Automates Prompt Injection Testing to Harden GPT-5.6 Sol
item.description: OpenAI has disclosed details of GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming model that scales prompt injection vulnerability discovery with an aim to fix issues before the tools are deployed...

item.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
item.description: Zoom has released security updates for a critical security flaw impacting Zoom Workplace for Windows that could facilitate account takeover. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-53412 (CVSS...

item.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
item.description: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

item.title: Skullcandy’s bass-boosting Crusher headphones now come with Bose’s ANC
item.description: Skullcandy announced a new version of its Crusher wireless headphones today featuring a few of Bose's audio technologies including its QuietControl ANC and head-tracking spatial audio. The Crusher...

item.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

item.title: HP’s OLED-equipped 2-in-1 laptop is a solid back-to-school deal
item.description: With storage and memory prices still at an all-time high, we’re happy to tell you about a solid deal on a good laptop when we find one, rare as they are. Best Buy is selling the HP OmniBook X Flip...

item.title: Apple’s reportedly raising the price for AppleCare Plus on Macs and iPads
item.description: An AppleCare Plus subscription for a Mac or iPad will cost more soon, with prices going up by $0.50 per month or $5 per year for new sign-ups while remaining the same for existing subscribers,...

item.title: Valve says iFixit will keep selling Steam Deck batteries after all
item.description: Valve has been incredibly friendly to customers who need repairs - which is why it was so surprising to hear that Valve was already discontinuing the battery for the Steam Deck LCD handheld. It...

item.title: Sheetz is quitting VMware, migrating 11,000 virtual machines
item.description: The convenience store chain will use StorMagic instead.

item.title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM ‘deepfakes’
item.description: The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In a lawsuit reported earlier by Reuters, xAI...

item.title: Brendan Carr plans to let broadcast giants dominate the airwaves
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission will vote next month on whether a single company can own broadcast stations that reach more than 39 percent of US TV households. In a Breitbart op-ed on...

item.title: Roblox is shutting down its video chat service
item.description: Roblox will be shutting down Roblox Connect, its video calling service introduced in 2023. Roblox Connect let you video chat with other people using your Roblox avatar, which would be able to...

item.title: AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
item.description: This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of The Odyssey. It's on track to rake in anywhere between...

item.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
item.description: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

item.title: TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance...

item.title: AI Isn’t Smarter Than a Baby—Yet
item.description: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.

item.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
item.description: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.
